noticeTemplate: This educational material references {work_title} by {authors}. All referenced content is used under Fair Use (17 U.S.C. § 107) for educational purposes. {additional_notes} We encourage you to purchase this work directly from {creator_link}.

additionalNotes.transformative: Content is transformed for interactive educational use, not reproduced verbatim. Skills are reframed as hands-on learning activities grounded in Inuit Qaujimajatuqangit (IQ) principles.
additionalNotes.amount: Only factual techniques, publicly shared knowledge, and ITK/community-authorized educational materials are referenced. Community-restricted knowledge is never reproduced.
additionalNotes.marketEffect: This educational pack actively encourages purchase of original works. Inhabit Media, the Inuit-owned publisher, and ITK publications are prioritized in all purchase links.
additionalNotes.purpose: Educational and non-commercial use for cultural preservation and heritage skills instruction, conducted with respect for Inuit self-determination and Inuit Qaujimajatuqangit.
additionalNotes.iqAlignment: All use of Inuit knowledge in this pack strives to align with Inuit Qaujimajatuqangit (IQ) principles, particularly Inuuqatigiitsiarniq (respecting others), Piliriqatigiinniq (collaborative relationships), and Qanuqtuurungnarniq (being resourceful). Only knowledge shared through community-authorized publications and Inuit-owned media is included.
additionalNotes.ocapCompliance: This pack acknowledges OCAP® Principles as the governing framework for all Indigenous knowledge included. Inuit-specific OCAP application is guided by ITK's National Inuit Strategy on Research (NISR), which frames Inuit research sovereignty and community consent requirements.

creatorSupportStatement: The Heritage Skills Educational Pack directs all learners to purchase original works from Inuit-owned publishers, particularly Inhabit Media (inhabitmedia.com) based in Iqaluit, Nunavut. Supporting Inhabit Media directly supports Inuit-controlled cultural publication and the preservation of Inuit knowledge in Inuit hands.
culturalSovereigntyStatement: Inuit peoples retain full sovereignty over Inuit Qaujimajatuqangit (IQ) — the comprehensive Inuit knowledge system that encompasses their worldview, values, and ways of living in relationship with the Arctic environment. The inclusion of Inuit-authorized educational materials in this pack does not constitute a claim of ownership or right of reproduction beyond the specific educational use described here. Inuit self-determination over Inuit knowledge is absolute.
ocapComplianceNote: This pack operates in accordance with the National Inuit Strategy on Research (NISR), established by ITK, which sets out Inuit priorities for how research is conducted in Inuit Nunangat and how Inuit knowledge is respected, attributed, and governed. We direct learners to ITK (itk.ca) for authoritative guidance on Inuit knowledge governance.
communityConsultationAcknowledgment: The selection and framing of Inuit knowledge in this educational pack was guided by ITK publications, Inhabit Media books, Government of Nunavut educational materials, and community-based participatory research with documented Inuit community involvement. We acknowledge that no set of published resources fully replaces direct relationship with Inuit communities, elders, and knowledge keepers. We encourage all learners to seek those direct connections through ITK's regional organizations: Nunatsiavut Government, Makivvik Corporation, Nunavut Tunngavik Inc., and the Inuvialuit Regional Corporation.
